references.title: A learning analytic approach to unveiling self-regulatory processes in learning tactics

extracted.Features: [Task Analysis, Goal Setting, Working on Task, Coordinating Learning Task Sequences, Evaluating]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors defined that a session would consist of an whole day. 

The labelling of the learning actions was not specifically focused on just counting of different actions on the LMS and, instead, mapped actions to specific learning actions, which would then be used to identify micro-level SRL processes. 

Micro-level processes were then associated to macro-level SRL behaviors.
extracted.Cluster Description: Authors obtain a matrix with the probabilities of transitions between learning actions. This will be the input
extracted.Comments: The first part of the work is to use FOOM to identify common sequences of actions. Because of information provided by instructors, the matrix is generated considering a whole day as a session.
extracted.Limitations:
Quality of the trace data

Definition of learning sessions

extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors defined that a session would consist of an whole day. 

The labelling of the learning actions was not specifically focused on just counting of different actions on the LMS and, instead, mapped actions to specific learning actions, which would then be used to identify micro-level SRL processes. 

Micro-level processes were then associated to macro-level SRL behaviors.
extracted.Cluster Description:
Tactic 1: Reading wuth Quizz - Reading oriented with an additional emphasis on Quizzes → the main tactic based even on the way the course was designed.

Tactic 2: Assessment and Interaction - Opposite of the first, almost 0 Reading. Mostly used to participate in assessments and interact with peers and teachers. 

Tactic 3: Short Login and Interact - The most frequent tactic of the course, with the shortest learning sessions → students login and check announcements.

Tactic 4: Focus on Quiz - Quiz related activities.
extracted.Implications:
Informing seems to be a relevant construct for all Learning Tactics that were identified.

Most micro-level processes adopted by students were part of engagement and evaluation… with planning being considerably less frequent. 

Learning actions can be converted into learning processes. 

Adoption of SRL-focused approaches may bridge the gap of “context-specific models and assist in the creation of more universal models”.
extracted.Comments:
The first part of the work is to use FOOM to identify common sequences of actions.

Authors obtain clusters and map the micro-SRL processes to the different sequences of actions. A MANOVA Test is conducted to show that all 5 micro-processes are statistically significant.
extracted.Limitations:
Quality of the trace data

Definition of learning sessions

annotations.text: Trace data has historically been used to identify different learning tactics. Authors propose a learning approach to identify and compare learning tactics. Authors identify 4 learning tactics in data from 728 learners and theorize five micro-processes.

Authors analyzed how those processese were activated when engaging with the 4 learning tactics. Analysis between learning strategies shows signifcant differences on the 5 micro-processes, showing that trace data has the potential to highlight different types of behaviors.
